title: How to create winter appeal in real estate
description: PROPERTY NEWS - As temperatures drop and...
bodyText:
PROPERTY NEWS - As temperatures drop and winter takes hold, REMAX Southern Africa encourages homeowners to transform their living spaces into warm, inviting sanctuaries.


> Whether you're staging a home for sale or simply seeking comfort during the colder months, creating a cosy winter interior can significantly enhance your space's appeal and functionality.


“By creating a home environment that feels warm, secure, and inviting, sellers can appeal directly to the emotional instincts of buyers. A home that feels good sells faster,” says Adrian Goslett, Regional Director and CEO of RE/MAX of Southern Africa.

Winter is the perfect season to create that warm and cosy emotional connection with buyers – if you play into it right.

Cozy Winter Décor Ideas:

- Add layers of texture with plush throws, soft scatter cushions, and thick rugs to suggest warmth and relaxation.
- Stick to a neutral palette complemented by earthy, warm tones such as ochre, burgundy, and olive for a look that is both elegant and seasonally appropriate.
- Replace harsh overhead lights with warm-toned bulbs and incorporate multiple light sources, such as table lamps and wall sconces, to soften the room.
- Indoor plants or floral arrangements, even in winter, can breathe life into a room and help counteract the greyness outside.
- Scent is an often-overlooked component of home staging. Introduce subtle winter fragrances (think spiced apple, cedarwood, or vanilla) through scented candles or diffusers.
- Don’t have a fireplace? Candles and fairy lights can also add an ambient glow that helps buyers envision a snug winter evening indoors.

Staging your home with winter-friendly design elements can make all the difference during the colder months. Cosy interiors invite potential buyers to imagine themselves enjoying the space, making them more likely to fall in love with the property.

“If you are unsure whether to stage your home before you sell, speak to your real estate professional for some free advice. Equipped with local market knowledge, they can help you make informed decisions that can give you the best chance to sell for full value,” Goslett concludes.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
